query: Iran Strait of Hormuz closure June 2026 status negotiations reopening
answer:
This is an extraordinarily detailed and well-sourced report. Let me compile the final structured output.

---

# Strait of Hormuz Crisis: Status Report (as of June 17, 2026)

## Overview

The Strait of Hormuz has been **actively closed since February 28, 2026**, following US-Israeli "Operation Midnight Hammer" strikes on Iran. This is the most significant energy supply disruption in modern history, now approximately 108 days old. A diplomatic breakthrough — the **Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding** — was digitally signed on June 14, 2026, with a formal ceremony scheduled for June 19 in Switzerland. However, actual physical reopening of the Strait remains deeply uncertain.

## Key Findings

### 1. Closure Status
- Iran closed the Strait on **February 28, 2026** in retaliation for Operation Midnight Hammer (coordinated US-Israeli strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and destroyed nuclear facilities at Fordow, Natanz, and Isfahan).
- On **June 10-11**, the IRGC formally declared "complete closure" and published expanded boundary maps extending into UAE territorial waters.
- **Commercial vessel traffic is effectively zero.** Approximately 2,000 ships from 87 countries and ~20,000 mariners are stranded.
- Pre-war, the Strait handled ~20.5 million barrels/day of crude oil and petroleum products (~25% of global seaborne oil, ~20% of global LNG).

### 2. Negotiations & the Islamabad MOU
- After multiple rounds of talks (Muscat, Geneva, Islamabad), a deal was reached via Pakistani and Qatari mediation.
- The **Islamabad MOU was digitally signed June 14**, with formal signing at **Buergenstock Resort, Switzerland on June 19**.
- Key reported terms include: end to military strikes, immediate Strait reopening (toll-free), full shipping restoration within ~30 days, end to the US naval blockade, and a 60-day ceasefire extension.
- However, the US and Iran are **publicly contradicting each other** on key provisions (investment fund, asset releases, toll/fee structure).
- VP Vance described the MOU as "a very general document" of roughly "a page and a half."

### 3. Obstacles to Reopening
- The **IRGC has not publicly endorsed** the deal — they declared "complete closure" just 3-4 days before the deal announcement.
- Insurance companies remain "largely unwilling" to cover Strait transit.
- Mine-clearing operations will be needed.
- **Fitch Ratings** assessed broad Hormuz reopening as unlikely before July 2026.
- **Prediction markets** show a 35-point gap: 75% probability of formal signing vs. only 40% probability of actual Strait reopening before June 27.

### 4. Oil Market Impact
- Brent crude surged from ~$72.50/bbl (pre-war) to a peak of **$126.10/bbl** (+74%), settling at ~$83.63/bbl post-deal announcement.
- US gasoline rose from $2.98/gallon to a peak of **$4.45/gallon** (+49%).
- Net global oil supply loss estimated at **~2 million bpd** (IEA).
- Iran's daily blockade losses estimated at **~$435 million/day**.
- Euro area energy inflation hit **10.9%** in April 2026.

### 5. Military Situation
- Three US Carrier Strike Groups deployed under "Project Freedom."
- US casualties: 15 killed, 543 wounded. Iranian casualties: 3,468+.
- US war cost: ~$27 billion through May 2026.
- Active strikes exchanged as recently as June 10-11.

key_facts: Iran has actively closed the Strait of Hormuz since February 28, 2026, following US-Israeli Operation Midnight Hammer strikes that killed Supreme Leader Khamenei and destroyed nuclear facilities
The closure is approximately 108 days old as of June 17, 2026, with commercial vessel traffic at effectively zero
Pre-war Strait throughput was ~20.5 million bpd of crude oil and petroleum products (~25% of global seaborne oil, ~20% of global LNG)
Approximately 2,000 ships from 87 countries and ~20,000 mariners are stranded
The IRGC declared 'complete closure' on June 10-11, extending boundary maps into UAE territorial waters
The Islamabad Memorandum of Understanding was digitally signed June 14, 2026, with formal signing scheduled June 19 at Buergenstock Resort, Switzerland
Deal mediated by Pakistan and Qatar; first direct US-Iran talks since 1979 occurred April 11-12 in Islamabad
MOU key terms reportedly include immediate Strait reopening without tolls, end to US naval blockade, 60-day ceasefire extension
US and Iran publicly contradict each other on key deal provisions (investment fund, asset releases, toll structure)
The IRGC has NOT publicly endorsed the deal despite operationally controlling the Strait closure
VP Vance described the MOU as 'a very general document' of roughly 'a page and a half'
Brent crude peaked at $126.10/bbl (+74% from pre-war $72.50), settling at ~$83.63 post-deal
US gasoline peaked at $4.45/gallon, up 49% from pre-war $2.98
Net global oil supply loss estimated at ~2 million bpd (IEA)
Three US Carrier Strike Groups deployed; US casualties 15 killed, 543 wounded; Iranian casualties 3,468+
Prediction markets show 75% probability of formal signing but only 40% probability of actual Strait reopening before June 27
Fitch Ratings assessed meaningful commercial traffic restoration as unlikely before July 2026
New Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei (selected March 8-9) has not made a verified public appearance in 99+ days
US war cost approximately $27 billion through May 2026
Iran's daily blockade losses estimated at ~$435 million/day
